title: rubia fruticosa subsp. periclymenum
intro: Rubia fruticosa subsp. periclymenum, also known as wild madder, is a perennial plant species in the madder family (Rubiaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of wild madder include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Wild madder can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to create red and pink dyes from its roots.
